{{Quote|In less than eight minutes, D'rinba IV shall cease to exist.|Darth Vader|<ref name="Star Wars: The Interactive Video Board Game">''[[Star Wars: The Interactive Video Board Game]]''</ref>}}
'''D'rinba IV''' was a [[Planet/Legends|world]] located in the [[Sombure sector/Legends|Sombure sector]] of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].

==History==
In the aftermath of the [[New Sith Wars]], during the [[Unification Campaigns]] of the [[900s BBY]], the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Judicial Forces/Legends|Judicial Forces]] [[Battle at D'rinba|fought]] ably at [[D'rinba system/Legends|D'rinba]], where they achieved a notable victory.<ref name="EGtW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>

During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], D'rinba IV that was home to a large [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] supply base targeted by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] during the relocation of the [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]] to Endor. When the D'rinba Rebels learned of Vader's intent, Rebel Alliance agent [[Corwin Shelvay/Legends|Corwin Shelvay]] organized a team consisting of [[Kyle Katarn]], [[Lumiya|Shira Brie]], [[Erling Tredway]], and up to two other agents to disable the [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]].

As the Death Star neared D'rinba IV, the Rebels attempted to evacuate, using an [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]] to disable the [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Colossus (Star Destroyer)|''Colossus'']]. The Rebels also used their fleet to [[Assault on the Death Star|assault]] the Death Star as Shelvay's operatives attempted to sabotage the weapon.

{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: The Interactive Video Board Game}}
The operatives were successful in preventing the superlaser's firing. However, the false data in possession of the main Rebel Fleet regarding the operational status of the weapon was never corrected, leading to a near-catastrophe at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Drinba_schematics.png|thumb|Schematics of D'rinba IV, showing geological formations]]
In the board-game video, D'rinba is represented by shots of [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]], [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], and [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] recycled from the [[original trilogy]].

If the players do not win the Interactive Video Board Game, Darth Vader destroys D'rinba IV. No source has established the [[canon]]ical outcome of the game's events, leaving the planet's survival ambiguous.

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: The Interactive Video Board Game]]'' {{1st}}
